name: Proteasome Enhancement field change check for June 7, 2026

scope.question: What changed for Proteasome Enhancement since the 2026-06-01 public baseline publication, and does any change justify a public update?

summary: Ran a field change check from the 2026-06-01T10:27:12.753Z Proteasome Enhancement baseline publication through June 7, 2026. The local trial audit found no scoped registry-linked human studies. Broad ClinicalTrials.gov searches found no proteasome-enhancing human aging program; close hits were unrelated aging/exercise records, amyloidosis or cancer proteasome-inhibitor studies, supplement or exercise programs, and proteostasis-adjacent disease records without a proteasome-enhancement intervention. PubMed searches found no correction or retraction to the baseline support set and no same-window human organismal or clinical proteasome-enhancement result. The closest source, PMID 41956660, reports fucoxanthin proteostasis and proteasome-activity effects in human senescent fibroblasts, hiPSC-derived neurons, and C. elegans Abeta-GFP models, but it was electronically published before the baseline and is not a human intervention result. Other close sources were skeletal-muscle UPS review context, Drosophila aggregation genetics, osteoarthritis chondrocyte mechanism, neurodegeneration reviews, or non-proteasome mitochondrial/parkin context. No public outlook, source, study, finding, activity, bundle, or coverage assessment update is warranted.

materiality_decision.rationale: The public baseline already frames this track as a low-firmness animal signal dominated by C. elegans lifespan/proteostasis results and cell-based human relevance. Current registry, PubMed, and preprint checks did not identify new human target engagement, clinical evidence, mammalian aging safety/benefit evidence, a correction/retraction, or a materially stronger post-baseline animal result. Pre-baseline fucoxanthin and review context can be considered in a future coverage assessment but should not be backfilled through ordinary surveillance.

search_log.summary: The audit found zero registry-linked interventional studies, zero missing detail records, zero stale registry checks, and zero open trial-watch items for the scoped track.

search_log.query: proteasome enhancement aging OR proteasome activator aging OR proteasome activation aging
search_log.summary: Returned three non-material records: whey-protein inflammaging and muscle metabolism, a Pythagorean self-awareness aging-biomarker intervention, and exercise in chronic heart failure and aging. None was a proteasome-enhancing intervention program.

search_log.query: (proteasome OR proteostasis) AND (aging OR older adult OR frailty OR Alzheimer OR Parkinson); and rolipram/cAMP-PKA/PSMD11/RPN6/PSME3/PA28gamma/18alpha-glycyrrhetinic acid terms with aging, Alzheimer, or neurodegeneration
search_log.summary: The broad registry screen returned mostly amyloidosis, oncology proteasome-inhibitor, ALS supplement, echo-analysis, nutrition, and observational records. The baseline-specific term screen returned zero records. No trial result or active proteasome-enhancement aging program was identified.

search_log.query: Proteasome, proteasomal, proteostasis, protein degradation, protein homeostasis, or proteotoxicity with enhancement, activation, activator, activity terms and aging, lifespan, longevity, healthspan, older-adult, senescence, or neurodegeneration terms; publication dates 2026-06-01 to 2026-06-07
search_log.summary: Returned 15 records. Close records included PMID 42119273, PMID 41956660, PMID 42249710, PMID 42249421, PMID 42243035, PMID 42031321, and PMID 41825655. They were review, pre-window electronic-publication, Drosophila aggregation, disease-cell, neurodegeneration, or non-proteasome mechanism context and did not change the public outlook.

search_log.query: RPN6, PSMD11, PA28gamma, PSME3, 18alpha-glycyrrhetinic acid, glycyrrhetinic acid, rolipram, cAMP-PKA, SKN-1A, NRF1, and open-gate proteasome terms with aging, lifespan, longevity, healthspan, proteostasis, aggregation, or neurodegeneration terms; publication dates 2026-06-01 to 2026-06-07
search_log.summary: Returned two same-window records, PMID 41833117 and PMID 41690071. Both were wrong-scope relative to proteasome enhancement: aging rooster Leydig-cell astaxanthin/mitochondrial function and an adenosine A2A receptor neuroinflammation review.

search_log.query: Baseline support PMIDs 20068043, 22922647, 26669444, 26692334, 26886723, 36007615, 37118550, and 41703241 with retraction, erratum, corrected-and-republished, or expression-of-concern publication types; publication dates 2026-06-01 to 2026-06-07
search_log.summary: Returned zero correction, retraction, erratum, corrected-and-republished, or expression-of-concern records for the eight baseline supporting PubMed sources.

search_log.query: Date window 2026-06-01 to 2026-06-07 with proteasome, proteostasis, RPN6, PSMD11, PSME3, PA28, glycyrrhetinic, rolipram, and SKN-1A title/abstract screens
search_log.summary: Returned zero matching bioRxiv or medRxiv title/abstract records in the date window.

excluded_sources.title: Fucoxanthin, a bioactive compound derived from edible seaweed, supports proteostasis and protects against stress and senescence.
excluded_sources.reason: Relevant proteostasis/proteasome activity source in human senescent fibroblasts, hiPSC-derived neurons, and C. elegans Abeta-GFP models, but electronically published on 2026-03-23 before the baseline and not a human intervention result. It is better handled as future coverage-assessment context.

excluded_sources.title: Dysregulation of the ubiquitin-proteasome system in aging skeletal muscle.
excluded_sources.reason: Same-window review of UPS regulation in aging skeletal muscle. It reinforces the need to avoid single-marker proteasome interpretations but adds no primary intervention evidence.

excluded_sources.title: Regulation of Huntingtin-polyQ Aggregation by Glucose Transporters and Saccharide-binding Receptors during Aging in Drosophila.
excluded_sources.reason: Drosophila huntingtin-polyQ aggregation genetics and saccharide-signaling source. It is proteostasis-adjacent but not a proteasome-enhancement intervention result.

excluded_sources.title: Multi-omics and experimental evidence in human chondrocytes identify caspase-8 as a non-apoptotic regulator of inflammatory, senescent, and fibrotic signaling in osteoarthritis.
excluded_sources.reason: Human osteoarthritis chondrocyte caspase-8 mechanism paper. Proteostasis-chromatin network language appears in proteomics, but the intervention and claim are not proteasome enhancement.

excluded_sources.title: Deubiquitinases at organelle quality control bottlenecks in neurodegeneration.
excluded_sources.reason: Review-level deubiquitinase and organelle-quality-control context. It may inform related UPS surveillance but does not add a proteasome-enhancement result.

excluded_sources.title: Co-aggregation of amyloidogenic proteins in age-related neurodegenerative diseases.
excluded_sources.reason: Review of protein co-aggregation and proteostasis modulators in age-related neurodegenerative disease. It is synthesis context without a direct proteasome-enhancement intervention result.

excluded_sources.title: Nuclear factor erythroid 2-related factor 2 induction abrogates mitochondrial stress through parkin regulation.
excluded_sources.reason: NRF2-parkin mitochondrial-stress source returned by proteostasis terms. It is Parkinson/mitochondrial-proteostasis biology rather than proteasome-enhancement evidence.

excluded_sources.title: Natural astaxanthin enhances testosterone synthesis by improving mitochondrial function and reducing oxidative stress in Leydig cells.
excluded_sources.reason: Aging rooster Leydig-cell mitochondrial/oxidative-stress source returned by NRF1 search terms. It is not proteasome enhancement.

excluded_sources.title: Adenosine A2A receptor as a dual-acting molecular switch: Glial morphological changes and neurovascular tissue remodeling in neuroinflammation and neurodegeneration.
excluded_sources.reason: A2A receptor neuroinflammation review returned by cAMP/PKA search terms. It is not a proteasome-enhancement intervention or aging result.

excluded_sources.title: Inflammaging and Muscle Protein Metabolism
excluded_sources.reason: Completed whey-protein older-adult muscle metabolism record returned by proteasome-aging registry terms. It is nutrition/protein metabolism context, not proteasome enhancement.

excluded_sources.title: Venetoclax, MLN9708 and dexamethasone for relapsed or refractory light chain amyloidosis
excluded_sources.reason: Active-not-recruiting amyloidosis regimen includes ixazomib, a proteasome inhibitor. It is the opposite direction and disease-specific oncology/amyloidosis context.

excluded_sources.title: The NO-ALS Study: Nicotinamide/Pterostilbene Supplement in ALS
excluded_sources.reason: ALS supplement trial returned by proteostasis/neurodegeneration terms. It does not test proteasome enhancement.

notes: Triage selected this as the rank-1 next distinct surveillance target after Lysosomal Restoration surveillance.
The delta window is anchored to publication event publish-proteasome-enhancement-bootstrap-2026-06-01-2026-06-01t10-27-12-753z.
No coverage assessment exists yet for Proteasome Enhancement; this ordinary surveillance pass did not discover a material category-level change requiring immediate coverage repair.

PMID 41956660 is the most relevant missed-context source found in this pass, but it was electronically available before the baseline and should be assessed in a future coverage backfill rather than staged through no-op surveillance.
The public boundary remains unchanged: direct organismal evidence is animal/worm-heavy, human relevance remains cell-based, and no clinical proteasome-enhancement program is visible.
next_actions: Continue ordinary surveillance for human proteasome target-engagement studies, mammalian aging studies with safety and function, or selective proteasome-enhancement clinical programs.
Consider a separate Proteasome Enhancement coverage assessment later to decide whether fucoxanthin and newer UPS aging-muscle review context should be represented in the public evidence layer.

Create a public bundle only if future evidence changes stage, evidence weight, momentum, blockers, safety, durability, human target engagement, functional outcomes, or proteasome-enhancement translation boundaries.
